# Extended Case References — Lecture MJs0XxC5_6Q

*Brief mentions of canonical cases that did not warrant a full `case_index.md` entry. Generated by reconciliation pass against the 1,676-cluster canon. Substantive treatments are in `case_index.md`.*

## Cases mentioned in passing

### High-strength steel cookware hydrogen cracking
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "High-strength steel cookware hydrogen cracking"
- **Anchor:** `§1.p3`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "So I said, you've got martensite, you've got your residual stresses from the forming operation — I don't know where your hydrogen comes from. And they said, well, could it have anything to do with the fact that we actually plasma-spray stainless steel powders onto the inside to make a porous surface so that we can impregnate — this is the top-of-the-line cookware — impregnate the non-stick coating plastic into this porous mass of stainless steel. I said, what's that got to do with hydrogen? Well, we use a 95 argon, 5 hydrogen gas mixture. Oh. So, case solved, okay."
